action.hypothesis: `lowrank_residual` just established the best `K=4, EF=12` point under the controlled low-K recipe, so the cleanest single-variable follow-up is to reduce only `K` from 4 to 2. If the same architecture transfers well one step lower, it can replace the current `K=2` frontier point from `lowrank_two_stage_residual` and extend the Pareto frontier toward smaller online cost.
action.summary: Run a pure-`K` transfer on `lowrank_residual` by keeping architecture, expansion factor, optimizer, learning rate, and aux fixed to the round-33 recipe and changing only `K` from `4` to `2`. This directly tests whether the newly validated ultra-low-K family can produce a better non-dominated `K=2` tradeoff.
action.self_review: This is the most policy-clean continuation from the latest successful run because it changes exactly one primary variable relative to round 33. It also matches the current memory guidance: after a `K=4` win, probe `K=2` before branching back into broader architecture exploration.
notes_to_memory: Round 34 continues the newly successful `lowrank_residual` low-K lane with a strict single-variable transfer from `K=4` to `K=2` under the same controlled `EF=12`, `signum`, `LR=1.6e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` recipe.

The goal is to test whether `lowrank_residual` can displace the current `K=2` frontier point and strengthen the ultra-low-K Pareto tail without changing execution semantics or introducing a new family.
next_hypotheses: If `lowrank_residual` wins at `K=2`, continue the same pure-`K` descent to `K=1`.
If it is close but slightly worse at `K=2`, keep the family active and consider a confirmatory rerun before changing another variable.
If it clearly loses at `K=2`, keep the `K=4` result and return to a different architecture branch rather than minor scalar sweeps on this tail.

result.description: Run a pure-`K` transfer on `lowrank_residual` by keeping architecture, expansion factor, optimizer, learning rate, and aux fixed to the round-33 recipe and changing only `K` from `4` to `2`. This directly tests whether the newly validated ultra-low-K family can produce a better non-dominated `K=2` tradeoff.
result.self_review: This is the most policy-clean continuation from the latest successful run because it changes exactly one primary variable relative to round 33. It also matches the current memory guidance: after a `K=4` win, probe `K=2` before branching back into broader architecture exploration.
